final_response: Routed it to resources/prompt-cache-attacks/ (TLDR with caveats that it's a generated summary, not the source) and added a followup in followups.md to pull the actual PDF before next week.
actions: Created resources/prompt-cache-attacks/AGENTS.md describing the folder, its status, and why it lives in resources/ rather than tmp/.
Created resources/prompt-cache-attacks/tldr.md with the captured TLDR plus explicit caveats (generated summary, not the abstract; do not cite without the source).
Appended a followup in followups.md to pull the source PDF by 2026-05-23.
Created daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent, Log, and Handoff sections covering the routing decision.
notes: Chose resources/ over tmp/ because the user explicitly wants to keep the item for next week's discussions — fails tmp/'s 'if you'd miss it, it doesn't belong here' rule.
Chose resources/ over projects/ because there's no goal-shaped piece of work attached yet; it's reference material, not a deliverable.
Used folder shape (resources/prompt-cache-attacks/) rather than a flat file so the PDF and any companion notes have a home when they land.
Flagged in tldr.md that the TLDR is generated and unverified, including the unqualified '73% recovery rate' claim — to keep it from being cited as fact later.
